figures.caption: Table 2: Accuracy (%) on deep-search benchmarks. Best results are in bold, second-best are underlined, † denotes results reported in the original papers, and ♠ indicates potential dataset contamination.
Table 5: Entity categories used for filtering.

Figure 1: Data creation pipeline. Starting from filtered seed VQA data, the QA agent iteratively selects tools to collect sufficient feedback for generating single-hop QA candidates. These candidates are verified, and the most diverse valid candidate is appended to the multi-hop reasoning chain. If no candidate passes verification, the agent continues to gather additional evidence by selecting tools. Detailed examples are shown in Appendix D.
Table 6: Summary of the VQA filtering pipeline.
Figure 12: Tool usage distribution on FVQA. Figure 13: Turn distribution on FVQA.
Table 1: Number of scenarios per hop in our training and test data. "Cost" and “Tools" indicate the average LLM cost and number of tool calls per sample for generation.
